mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate and reduces the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains below 0.5%.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or mean forward return during sub-0.5% FEDFUNDS periods is not significantly positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, labor market deterioration is severe enough to trigger risk-off repricing of equities as investors anticipate weaker consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following UNRATE crossing 6.0% are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a broad-based consumer and residential investment retrenchment that is not captured by either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST decline signal produces forward 60d returns statistically indistinguishable from single-series RSAFS-only signal, indicating no incremental interaction effect (interaction coefficient not significant at 10% level).

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the federal funds rate is near zero but CPI inflation is simultaneously rising, markets price in future forced tightening, creating a stagflation-risk discount on equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
regime_hint: zero-rate regime with rising inflation (stagflation risk)
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS<0.5% and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change >3%, forward 20d SPX returns are not significantly negative relative to baseline zero-rate periods without inflation acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding real economic output and corporate revenue capacity, which supports gradual upward repricing of equities over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month INDPRO growth and forward 60d SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 0.3% signals an abrupt labor market contraction that historically precedes broad equity market drawdowns as recession risk is repriced, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following PAYEMS declines exceeding 0.3% are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero while unemployment remains elevated above 5.5%, the joint condition signals a policy mismatch consistent with early-recovery slack, and the anticipated persistence of accommodative policy through the labor cycle produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: early post-crisis recovery with policy accommodation and labor slack
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns conditioned on joint low-rate/high-unemployment regime are not significantly different from returns conditioned on low FEDFUNDS alone, indicating no interaction premium.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate regime, a deceleration in real GDP growth signals fragile recovery momentum, causing short-horizon equity weakness as investors reassess the pace of earnings recovery within the next trading week.
failure_condition: During zero-rate periods, quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 deceleration shows no statistically significant negative relationship with forward 5d SPX returns (p>0.10) across historical sample.
